source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A thin, uniform, \(3.80\text{ kg}\) bar, \(80.0\text{ cm}\) long, has very small balls glued on at either end as shown in figure, It is supported horizontally by a thin, horizontal, frictionless axle passing through its center and perpendicular to the bar. Suddenly the right-hand ball becomes detached and falls off, but the other ball remains glued to the bar. Find the angular velocity of the bar just as it swings through its vertical position.

image_dependency: question explicitly says "as shown in the figure"
the text gives the bar mass and length but does not state the ball values, so essential setup details appear to be in the image
multi_step: solving requires combining information from the diagram with the text
the task is not a direct lookup; it involves rotational dynamics/energy reasoning from horizontal to vertical motion
the final angular velocity depends on multiple physical quantities (bar properties, attached ball, axle through center)
verifiability: answer_type is short_text with a specific numeric target: "5.70 rad/s"
multiple-choice options are provided, giving an additional consistency check
asset registry passed and answer is present

decision_reasons: Question, answer, and primary image are all present, so the sample retains recoverable core semantics.
The problem is highly image-dependent, and the scoring record indicates some essential diagram information is not fully transcribed in the text.
The available image is small (294x95), which raises follow-up/recovery risk even though the asset exists.
Given the collection-stage high-recall policy, this should remain in the candidate pool but below fully robust samples.
